artPrompt: A weary middle-aged human clerk in shirtsleeves and loosened tie, sleeves rolled, balanced on a wheeled ladder against a towering cliff of filing cabinets, one arm outstretched mid-reach for a blank folder. Cold cyan light rakes from below, casting his long dramatic shadow up the receding drawers; dust motes drift in the fluorescent haze. Low-angle foreshortening makes the cabinet wall loom enormous, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Junior Filing Clerk
text: When Junior Filing Clerk enters the battlefield, scry 1. (He alphabetizes it for you.)
flavorText: He has been here eleven years and is still, somehow, the junior.
